question:
Will Elon Musk post 360-379 tweets from November 4 to November 11, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 4 12:00 PM ET to November 11, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

evidences.title: Twitter under Elon Musk - Wikipedia
evidences.content: A computational analysis published on November 1, 2024, found potential algorithmic bias on X in favour of Republican Party "Republican Party (United States)") supporters. The study, which looked at more than 56,000 posts, identified a system-wide "structural break" on the platform around July 13, 2024, a date which coincides with Elon Musk's endorsement of Donald Trump in the 2024 US presidential election. From that point on, Elon Musk and other Republican commentators saw increased visibility for their posts. According to the researchers behind the study, this "could imply an algorithmic adjustment". On January 17, 2025, the European Commission requested to see X's internal documentation about its algorithms "and any recent changes made to it", following accusations of manipulation [...] 66. ↑ MCDONALD, ANDREW; WEBBER, ESTHER (August 7, 2024). evidences.content: Furthermore, Twitter lists timestamps in the GMT timezone. For all figures except those that explicitly require timestamps and are sensitive to exact hour/time, I convert these timestamps to CST. That corresponds to the timezone in Texas, where Elon resides and spends the majority of his time. Ultimately, I was able to gather data from 7/27/24 to 9/12/24 (48 days). #### 2 thoughts on “Elon Musk definitely tweets too much” 1. Image 10 ArcosJanuary 7, 2025 at 6:13 pm Reply Nice work! From 7/27/24 to 9/12/24, he hasaveraged nearly 68 tweets per day. I estimate that hespends ~2.7 hours/day on Twitterusing conservative assumptions. He constantly interacts with notoriously racist alt-right accounts, like Ian Miles Cheong (@stillgray) and EndWokeness (@endwokeness) For better or worse, Elon Musk is one of the richest and most powerful men on the planet. evidences.title: How Traders Are Making Thousands Counting Elon Musk's Tweets
evidences.content: There's a market on Poly Market right now with $23 million in volume, $15 million traded in the last 24 hours. And it's not about war. It's not about an election. It's not even about crypto. It's about how many times Elon Musk is tweeting this week. 30 different brackets, 0 to 19 tweets, 20 to 39 all the way up to 580 plus. Thousands of people spending real money trying to predict the posting habits of one man. And somehow this might be the smartest market on the entire platform. Let me explain. So here's how it works. Every week, Poly Market opens a new market. How many times will Elon Musk post on X from Monday to Monday? Posts, quote tweets, and reposts all count. Replies do not, unless they show up on his main feed. The market splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. [...] splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. You buy the bracket you think he lands in, and if you're right, your share pays out a dollar. If you're wrong, they go to zero. Right now, the current week, February 24th to March 3rd, the market is pricing 300 to 319 tweets as the most likely outcome at 24%. The bracket below it, 280 to 299 is 22% and 320 to 339 is also 22%. So the m...

evidences.title: X's audience shifts right without losing reach
evidences.content: X has seen a shift to the right in its audience, without losing overall reach, according to our Digital News Report 2025. Here are three things to know about this issue: 1️⃣ Usage of X for news is stable or increasing across many markets. The biggest uplift is in the United States (+8 points for news from last year to 23%), Australia and Poland ( both +6). 2️⃣ Since Elon Musk bought the social network in 2022, many more right-leaning people, notably young men, have flocked to it, while some progressive audiences have left or are using it less frequently. Twitter pre-2022 was populated mostly by those on the progressive side of the political spectrum, but the proportion that self-identifies on the right has grown after Elon Musk took over. 3️⃣ In the US, last year’s presidential election [...] Kill the sandbox that no longer fits the vision. The goal is to frame X as a new category: an AI-powered, creator-first, all-in-one platform. A digital infrastructure company, not a media brand. Think less “tweet,” more “digital operating system.” We can also assume a trend here: this is also an Elon playbook move. He’s not building a product. evidences.title: X under Musk's leadership: Substantial hate and no reduction in ...
evidences.content: controlling for overall repost popularity. For any account that posts at least ten reposts, we record the ID of all reposts then create a TF-IDF vector by calculating each repost ID’s “term frequency” (how often it appears for each account) multiplied by their inverse document frequency (how many accounts share this repost). Unlike, e.g., simple repost frequency, TF-IDF gives greater weight to reposts that appear unusually often among a few accounts and less weight to reposts that are popular and broadly shared across X. Accounts whose vectors have a cosine similarity in the top 0.1 percentile are considered coordinated. Finally, activity similarity is defined as accounts posting (reposting or writing original posts, replies, or quote posts) nearly simultaneously. Following previous work [...] in posts that, while hostile, did not directly target an identity group. Our results are robust to this change. The posts were collected at five randomly selected five-minute intervals per day using the Twitter API for Academic Research (now the X API). We then use the Perspective API to remove posts with toxicity confidence below 0.7 and with sexually explicit confidence above 0.3. [...]...

By Kara Manke An overlay of the Twitter logo and the X logo Janine Schmitz/picture-alliance/dpa/AP Images February 13, 2025 A new analysis has found that weekly rates of hate speech on the social media platform X rose about 50% in the months after its purchase in October 2022 by Elon Musk, and that the number of bots and bot-like accounts did not decrease, despite Musk’s earlier pledge to reduce bot activity. [...] Because information on specific internal changes at X is limited, it is not possible to attribute the changes in hate speech or bot activity to any specific policy implemented during Musk’s tenure. This decision follows a major outage on X and growing concern from investors about his political involvement taking time away from his companies. After the outage, Musk said on X that staying “super focused” is key as his companies work on big projects like Starship, Tesla updates, and xAI. His political ties also led to anti-Tesla protests and a drop in the company’s stock. evidences.title: karcharodon/musk-tweet-tracker
evidences.content: # Musk Tweet Tracker — Polymarket Automation Automated toolkit for trading Elon Musk tweet count markets on Polymarket. Fetches live data from xtracker.io (official resolution source) and Polymarket API, analyzes tweet patterns, forecasts totals, and builds optimal position structures. ## How It Works Polymarket hosts prediction markets on how many times Musk will tweet over a given period (2-day and 7-day windows). The market is split into ranges (e.g. "201-225 tweets"). You build a structure of positions across ranges — if the actual count lands within your coverage, you profit. This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure [...] This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure `config.py — Configuration and constants polymarket_client.py — Polymarket API client (Gamma + CLOB) tweet_tracker.py — xtracker.io scraper with local history analyzer.py — Statistical analysis: rolling avg, wave detection, forecast position_builder.py — Position structure optimizer trade_logger.py — CSV trade journal with analytics main.py — CLI dashboard tying everything together` ## Quick Start ### Modes ## Strategy Rules (encoded in config) ## API Reference | API | Base URL | Au...

Mr. Musk has also served as Chief Executive Officer, Chief Technology Officer and Chairman of Space Exploration Technologies Corporation, a company which develops and launches advanced rockets and spacecraft (“ SpaceX ”), since May 2002, served as Chairman of the Board of a solar installation company, SolarCity, from July 2006 until its acquisition by us in November 2016, served as Chief Technology Officer of X Corp, a social media company (“ X”), since October 2022 and served as the Chief Executive Officer and Treasurer of x.AI Corp, an artificial intelligence company (“ xAI ”), since March 2023. Following [...] Number of Shares : 96 million shares of common stock. Vesting Schedule : The 2025 CEO Interim Award will vest upon the second anniversary of August 3, 2025, subject to Mr. Musk remaining in continuous service as CEO or as an executive officer responsible for product development or operations (as approved by the Board’s disinterested directors) through such second anniversary. Early Forfeiture : The 2025 CEO Interim Award will be immediately forfeited and returned to the...

evidences.title: Elon Musk | SpaceX, Tesla, xAI, X, & PayPal | Britannica Money
evidences.content: SpaceX developed the Starlink satellite network to provide broadband internet globally. Musk launched the project in 2015 as part of his broader Mars ambitions. In March 2018, the FCC approved launches of 4,425 satellites into low Earth orbit, and the first batch launched in May 2019. Beta testing began in 2020, followed by commercial service in 2021.
